title: oxalis doceana
intro: Oxalis doceana, also known as the pink-scented wood sorrel, is a species of flowering plant in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its astringent, anti-inflammatory, and antibacterial effects. The health benefits of Oxalis doceana include reducing fever, treating wounds, and alleviating digestive issues, due to its high concentration of oxalic acid. Oxalis doceana can be prepared as a tea, tincture, or salve for topical application, and is also used in traditional medicine to make a poultice for skin conditions.
